sections.title: Documentation
sections.desc: FASTDocs is a front-end only documentation layout
sections.descLC: fastdocs is a front-end only documentation layout

sub.text:
introduction
    fastdocs is a documentation layout for quickly creating a documentation from scratch. the advantages of
      fastdocs are:
    
    
      it has built-in indexing and search functionality.
      side-bar contents and site map are generated automatically.
      asset injection.
      it is front-end only, no need to meddle with server-side code.
      it is debranded and universal.
    
    fastdocs uses section html tag data-title, data-tags and
      data-desc attributes to index and separate and between contents. everything can be done without
      writing a single line of javascript or server-side code.
sub.title: Introduction

sub.text:
usage
    first download the project (this documentation) from github, then delete map.json and all the
      sections inside sections directory, this will give you clean workspace.
    later, write your own sections in pure html, put it in sections directory or any appropriate place
      you like.
    lastly, edit load.json file, add file path of newly created sections, and edit document title,
      title separator... to your liking.
    now you can start using your own documentation fastdocs will handle the rest, optionally a new site map
      (map.json) can be exported for increased efficiency.
sub.title: Usage

sub.text:
creating sections writing your own sections are easy as writing normal html code. create a new pure html file without head and body tags and start writing your contents as you would normally do. for content separation, fastdocs utilizes section tag. these section tags alongside with any content inside of them then will be processed and mapped out for side-bar contents, search functionality and indexing. there are 3 attributes needed to be defined inside section tags for processing and differentiation. these attributes are: data-title: title of your section, the title will be shown in side-bar and in search results. data-tags: tags of your section, tags are for searching functionality, add keywords related to your section for them to be searched easily. in order to add multiple tags, seperate them with a comma (,) inside the attribute. data-desc: description of your section, brief explanation to be shown in search results for your section. multiple sections can be inside one another to represent sub-sections. a healthy section with a sub-section should look like this:

title

lorem ipsum doler sit amet.

sub-section

lorem ipsum doler sit amet.
sub.title: Creating Sections
sub.desc: Write your own sections in FASTDocs
sub.descLC: write your own sections in fastdocs

sub.text:
external sections
      sections can be separated into different files and can be loaded from inside one another (instead of just
        load.json) with data-src attribute that contains the url of the section to be retrieved.
      
      <section data-src="path/to/section.html"></section>
sub.title: External Sections

sub.text:
site map
    site map grants additional efficiency for indexing. without a map, fastdocs has to load every section first in order to index them, which is not ideal.
    with a site map, fastdocs only loads the site map as initial process, and sections are loaded only when needed to show them.
    map.json can be exported via download  button on top navigation bar (don't forget to delete old map.json first).
sub.title: Site Map
sub.desc: Export site map for increased efficiency
sub.descLC: export site map for increased efficiency

sub.text:
asset injection, theming, scripting
    fastdocs already provides you with a sufficient and responsive user interface, however it is also possible to stylize your documentation with stylesheets or even execute scripts within sections.
    injecting styles and scripts are easy as you normally would do. if you want to add a style or script just add them to your sections using style, link, script tags. your assets will be loaded when section is showed.
    if you want to inject assets globally, insert your styles and scripts to assets.html file. now your assets will be loaded at the initial process. fastdocs will handle the assets automatically.
    one thing to consider while injecting assets is that; fastdocs ships with fontawesome icons and custom stylesheet, so beware of clashing styles.
sub.title: Asset Injection, Theming, Scripting
sub.desc: Injecting styles or scripts in FASTDocs
sub.descLC: injecting styles or scripts in fastdocs

sub.title: docs.onSectionLoad()
sub.desc: Execute script when sections are loaded
sub.descLC: execute script when sections are loaded
